title: Does permanent extensional deformation in lower forearc slopes indicate shallow plate-boundary rupture?
abstract: The paper documents permanent extensional deformation of the lower slope localized to the region 20.8°S–22°S along the North Chilean margin. Critical taper analysis, comparison of permanent deformation to inter-seismic seismicity and plate-coupling models, and observations from other subduction zones including the 2011 Tohoku-Oki area suggest that the normal faults at the lower slope may have resulted from shallow, possibly near-trench breaking earthquake ruptures. The study used multibeam bathymetry and legacy seismic reflection lines to define the deformation pattern.

location.description: Lower forearc slope offshore north Chile (20.8°–22°S); extensional deformation study

seismic_lines.name: SO104-07 — lower slope ~21°S, CINCA95 reprocessed at Barcelona-CSI
SO104-09 — lower slope ~22°S, CINCA95 reprocessed at Barcelona-CSI

data.name: Multibeam Bathymetry — North Chile Lower Slope 20.8°–22°S
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry revealing permanent extensional deformation (normal faults) of the lower slope at 20.8°–22°S; data from recent GEOMAR cruises. [SO211 cruise datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Legacy MCS Profiles — North Chile Lower Slope 20.8°–22°S
data.description: Legacy seismic reflection lines used to characterize subsurface structure of the deformed lower slope at 20.8°–22°S, complementing new bathymetric mapping. [CINCA95 cruise (SO104, 1995) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Multibeam bathymetry from SONNE cruises SO104 and SO244 — north Chile
Multibeam backscatter data from SONNE cruise SO244

_data_note: Same dataset as Geersen et al. 2018 Tectonics paper. EPSL companion study focused on extensional deformation at 20.8°–22°S.

processing.summary: New multibeam bathymetry and legacy MCS reflection lines from the north Chilean margin (20.8°–22°S) document permanent extensional deformation of the lower forearc slope. Critical taper analysis and comparison with plate-coupling models support interpretation of the normal faults as evidence of shallow, near-trench earthquake rupture. Companion to Geersen et al. 2018 Tectonics study.
workflow: 4. Comparison with seismicity and coupling models
processing.notes: EPSL paper; companion to Geersen et al. 2018 Tectonics paper

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. Open access CC BY-NC-ND; open_access_url set. Authors 10, all confirmed correct. Seismic data: SO104 (CINCA95, BGR, 1995): ~3124 cui airgun array, 3km/120ch/25m streamer; reprocessed at Barcelona-CSI with Claritas (decon, f-k, radon multiple attenuation, PreSTM, PSTM+PSDM). Bathymetry: SO244 (2015). Named lines SO104-07 and SO104-09. year_acquired corrected 2014→1995. vessel updated to SO104+SO244. source_volume_cui=3124, streamer=3000m, channels=120. Software corrected to Claritas+GMT.
